page: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the memory-intensive FXmsv2 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs. Virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark results highlight a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Due to the high 4.2 GHz clock speed, the server achieves top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2333.0 and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 34.85 GB/sec. However, because of the low core count of 1 physical core, multi-core performance is limited, with a poor stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 2215.28 and a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 2897.0. Memory bandwidth is also relatively constrained, with large-block read bandwidth measured at a poor 21.59 GB/sec and write bandwidth at a weak 14.36 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4-2ms_v2 offers a specialized hardware profile that trades off multi-core processing power and local storage for massive memory density per vCPU and high single-threaded clock speeds. This makes it a highly specialized option rather than a general-purpose resource. The server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallelization but benefit from high single-core speed, such as small-scale in-memory databases, single-threaded data processing tasks, text processing, and lightweight caching layers.
description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz, paired with 84.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage and no GPUs. Benchmarks demonstrate top-tier single-core performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2333.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds, contrasted by weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to the low core count. Qualitatively, this server trades parallel processing power for high memory density and single-thread speed. It is best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, small in-memory databases, and text processing applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 84 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz CPU, built for single-threaded, high-memory tasks.
tagline: A high-memory virtual machine featuring exceptional single-core clock speeds and massive memory density for specialized, single-threaded workloads.

84.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU resources included
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads
